title: scrophularia canina subsp. juratensis
intro: Scrophularia canina subsp. juratensis, also known as Cornish figwort, is a subspecies of the figwort plant, a perennial herb native to the UK. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cornish figwort include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and coughs. Cornish figwort can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a versatile remedy for those seeking natural alternatives to conventional medications.
